title: Create shared Zephyr app-interface event contract
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-9c4y.2 contract work found that gateway_inbound_events is app-local and Meshtastic-specific fanout is hardwired, while MeshCore, Meshtastic, Native, CoAP, and future app surfaces need the same text/status/config/local-info boundary. What needs to be done: design and implement a small Zephyr-safe lichen_app_interface subsystem or shared library for app ingress/egress events, local info, config/status access, queue/drop semantics, and adapter registration without creating app-specific coupling. Acceptance criteria: Meshtastic and MeshCore adapters can share the event contract; gateway_inbound_* either moves behind it or delegates to it; native_sim tests cover text/status event fanout and unsupported/no-subscriber behavior; no RF protocol bypass is introduced.
notes:
Implementation evidence 2026-07-01: added shared Zephyr lichen_app_interface subsystem with bounded text/status event structs, sink registration/unregistration, callback snapshot fanout, deterministic no-subscriber -ENOTSUP, payload bound checks, backpressure/subscriber-error stats, locked stats copy API, and single-owner status/config provider hooks. Gateway inbound text/status wrappers now lazily register the existing Meshtastic bridge as an app-interface sink, preserving gateway_inbound_* compatibility and existing Meshtastic FromRadio output. Meshtastic and MeshCore BLE product Kconfig paths both select LICHEN_APP_INTERFACE; actual MeshCore sink use is intentionally left to dependent project-LICHEN-9c4y.8 now that it has this shared contract.

Review evidence: Kierkegaard the 2nd initial review found P1 unregister lifetime ambiguity, P2 partial fanout errors hidden from stats, and P2 direct mutable stats pointer. Fixed by documenting unregister as non-quiescing with caller-owned stable lifetime, recording partial fanout errors even when another sink succeeds, and replacing direct stats pointer with lichen_app_interface_copy_stats(). Gauss the 2nd initial review found P2 MeshCore product path did not select LICHEN_APP_INTERFACE and P2 missing config/status provider hooks. Fixed by selecting the subsystem from MeshCore BLE and adding get_status/get_config/set_config provider callbacks plus tests. Kierkegaard follow-up found P2 config split-brain risk from multi-provider set_config; fixed with single-owner provider conflict checks returning -EALREADY and test_provider_hooks_are_single_owner. Final follow-ups from Kierkegaard and Gauss found no remaining P0/P1/P2.

Validation on EC2 instance i-05e27e074db786e22 in clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-9c4y4: git diff --check PASS. Direct native_sim app_interface: west build -p always -b native_sim lichen/tests/app_interface -d build-app-interface -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen && west build -d build-app-interface -t run PASS, app_interface 10/10. Direct native_sim meshtastic_gateway_adapter: west build -p always -b native_sim lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -d build-meshtastic-gateway-adapter -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen && west build -d build-meshtastic-gateway-adapter -t run PASS, 11/11. Twister: python3 /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN/zephyr/scripts/twister -T lichen/tests/app_interface -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -p native_sim --inline-logs --outdir twister-out-app-interface --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen PASS, 2/2 configurations, 21 cases, 0 skipped, 0 warnings. Product builds: Meshtastic-enabled gateway native_sim build PASS with /tmp/meshtastic_gateway.conf; MeshCore-enabled gateway native_sim build PASS with /tmp/meshcore_gateway.conf. Notable warnings: native_sim eth_posix zeth warning in Meshtastic gateway ztest, CONFIG_BT_FIXED_PASSKEY warning for MeshCore product build tracked by project-LICHEN-9c4y.17, and pre-existing Zephyr no-sources warnings for loramac-node/drivers__net/drivers__spi.
close_reason: Completed: added shared Zephyr app-interface subsystem with text/status event fanout, deterministic no-subscriber/backpressure/error semantics, locked stats copy, single-owner status/config provider hooks, Meshtastic gateway wrapper delegation, and Kconfig selection from Meshtastic and MeshCore BLE product modes. EC2 validation passed: app_interface 10/10, meshtastic_gateway_adapter 11/11, Twister 2/2 configs with 21 cases and 0 warnings, Meshtastic and MeshCore gateway native_sim product builds PASS. Reviews: all P1/P2 findings fixed; final reviewers reported no P0/P1/P2 remaining.
